assertion.text: The amyloid cascade explains a sufficiently large fraction of Alzheimer's disease architecture that no competing single-mechanism hypothesis can displace it. Predictions: A systematic comparison of mechanistic hypotheses (tau-first, neuroinflammation, vascular, metabolic) against AD pathological features would show amyloid covering more features than any single alternative.; Combination therapies pairing anti-amyloid agents with a second mechanism should outperform monotherapy in slowing cognitive decline.

evidence_spans.text: Note asserts amyloid 'still explains too much of the disease architecture to fully discard' as the reason for its persistence.

provenance.title: Amyloid cascade hypothesis remains dominant despite clinical failures.md
